sectionsModified: Content access model (sealed class description replaces value-like type)
Manage roles programmatically — info callout (added bold title)
Update role bindings — MakePublic code comment and prose paragraph (accuracy fix)
ASP.NET Identity integration — info callout (added bold title)
docFactsUsed: AREA-002: ContentAccessSettings struct with static factory methods Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<int>). IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings> implementation. IsSecured and RequiredMemberRoleIDs properties.
AREA-002: ContentItemMemberRoleManager.UpdateBindings() only manages ContentItemMemberRoleInfo records (binding table). Does NOT update ContentItemIsSecured flag.

AREA-006: IContentQueryDataContainer.ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames: IReadOnlyCollection<string>. ContentItemRolesResultProcessor post-processes query results. Lazy loading when ContentItemIsSecured=true.
AREA-009: ContentItemFieldsSourceExtensions.HasAccess(this IContentItemFieldsSource, ClaimsPrincipal) — returns bool. Internal ContentAccessCheckResult enum: Allowed, NotAuthenticated, Forbidden.
notes: Attempt 2 rewrite addressing all reviewer feedback from attempt 1. Changes: (1) STYLE/INV-frontmatter-004: redirect_from changed from empty [] to x/member_roles_xp matching sibling page pattern. (2) STYLE/INV-callout-002: Added bold titles to both info callouts — 'Internal namespace' for CMS.ContentEngine.Internal callout, 'Role assignment' for UserManager callout. (3) STYLE/INV-structure-001: Added '## Result' section before Next steps summarizing what the reader achieves. (4) ACCURACY: Fixed incorrect claim 'marks the item as public' — UpdateBindings() only manages the binding table, does NOT update ContentItemIsSecured flag. Changed prose to 'removes all member role bindings' and added note that fully reverting to public also requires setting IsSecured=false. Updated MakePublic() code comment accordingly. (5) PERSONA: Changed 'value-like type' to 'sealed class with static factory methods and value-like equality semantics' for precision about ContentAccessSettings being a sealed class.
